title: desmodium oxyphyllum var. mandshuricum
intro: Desmodium oxyphyllum var. mandshuricum, also known as Manchurian tick trefoil, is a perennial legume native to East Asia, characterized by its small leaves and pink or purple flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Desmodium oxyphyllum var. mandshuricum are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
